Container.Name: BitBonsai
Container.Overview:
BitBonsai - Intelligent Video Encoding Platform with Multi-Node Distribution
🎬 FEATURES: • Smart Codec Detection - Automatically converts H.264 → H.265/AV1 • Hardware Acceleration - NVIDIA NVENC, Intel QSV, AMD AMF, Apple M-Series • Multi-Node Encoding - Distribute jobs across multiple workers (LXC, Docker, bare metal) • Automatic Storage Sharing - NFS auto-detection and mounting for MAIN/LINKED nodes • Resume Capability - Pick up where you left off after interruptions • Real-Time Progress - Live FFmpeg progress with ETA and speed tracking • Auto-Healing - Automatically retry failed jobs with exponential backoff • SSD Cache Support - Use Unraid cache pool for 10-100x faster encoding • Container Health Monitoring - Automatic detection of problematic files • Job Delegation - Manual or automatic job distribution to specific nodes • SSH Key Exchange - Seamless multi-node setup with automatic key sharing
📊 STORAGE SAVINGS: • H.264 → H.265: 40-60% smaller files • H.264 → AV1: 50-70% smaller files • Same visual quality with advanced encoding profiles
🔧 PERFECT FOR: • Plex/Jellyfin/Emby media servers • *arr stack automation (Sonarr/Radarr/Whisparr integration) • Large media libraries (1000s of files) • Home servers and NAS systems • Multi-node homelab setups

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Web interface port - Access BitBonsai UI at http://[IP]:8108. Single port serves both frontend and backend API. Port 8108 chosen for zen/bonsai aesthetic.

Config.Name: Media Library
Config.Description: Your main media library (Movies, TV Shows). BitBonsai will scan for videos and encode according to your policies. Supports nested directories and multiple folders.

Config.Name: Downloads Folder
Config.Description: Downloads folder for *arr stack integration. BitBonsai can auto-detect and encode new downloads from Sonarr/Radarr/Whisparr. Leave empty if not using *arr apps.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Database and configuration storage. Contains SQLite database (bitbonsai.db), job history, and application state. IMPORTANT: Backup this folder regularly!

Config.Name: Encoding Cache (SSD)
Config.Description: CRITICAL FOR PERFORMANCE: Temporary workspace for encoding operations. Using an SSD cache drive provides 10-100x faster encoding performance vs HDD array. BitBonsai writes temp files here during encoding, then moves final output to /media.

Config.Name: Admin Password
Config.Description: REQUIRED: Set a strong admin password for web UI access. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 24. Leave empty to generate random password on first start (check container logs).

Config.Name: JWT Secret
Config.Description: REQUIRED: Secret key for JWT token signing. Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32. Leave empty to generate random secret on first start (check container logs).

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions (99 = nobody on Unraid). Files created by BitBonsai will use this UID.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions (100 = users on Unraid). Files created by BitBonsai will use this GID.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone for logging timestamps and scheduled jobs. Use IANA timezone format (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London).

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Logging verbosity: debug (very detailed), info (normal), warn (warnings only), error (errors only). Use debug for troubleshooting.

Config.Name: Max Concurrent Jobs
Config.Description: Maximum number of encoding jobs running simultaneously. Guidelines: CPU-only=1, Single GPU=2-3, Multi-GPU=4-6. Higher values require more RAM and temp storage.

Config.Name: Nice Level
Config.Description: CPU priority (-20 to 19). Lower = higher priority. Default 10 keeps encoding as background task. Use 0 for max speed, 19 for minimal system impact.

Config.Name: NVIDIA GPU (NVENC)
Config.Description: Enable NVIDIA GPU hardware encoding. Set to 'all' for all GPUs, or specific GPU UUID (nvidia-smi -L). Requires Unraid NVIDIA plugin and '--runtime=nvidia' in Extra Parameters below.

Config.Name: Intel QuickSync (QSV)
Config.Description: Enable Intel QuickSync hardware encoding. Set to '/dev/dri' to pass all render devices. Requires Intel CPU with integrated graphics (6th gen or newer recommended).

Config.Name: AMD GPU (AMF)
Config.Description: Enable AMD GPU hardware encoding. Set to '/dev/dri/renderD128' (or renderD129 for second GPU). Requires AMD GPU with AMF support (RX 400 series or newer).

Config.Name: Node Role
Config.Description: Node type in multi-node setup: MAIN (central database + job distribution) or LINKED (worker node). For single-node installations, use MAIN.

Config.Name: Main Node URL
Config.Description: For LINKED nodes only: URL of MAIN node (e.g., http://192.168.1.100:4210). Leave empty for MAIN nodes. Used for job fetching and status reporting.

Config.Name: Node Name
Config.Description: Custom name for this node (e.g., 'Encoding-Server-01', 'GPU-Node-RTX3090'). Leave empty to auto-generate from hostname. Displayed in web UI.

Config.Name: Has Shared Storage
Config.Description: Does this node have access to shared NFS/SMB storage? Set to 'true' if /media is mounted from NFS/SMB. Set to 'false' to enable file transfer mode (rsync over SSH).

Config.Name: NFS Exports
Config.Description: For MAIN nodes: Comma-separated NFS exports to share (e.g., '/media:/mnt/user/media,/downloads:/mnt/user/Downloads'). Automatically shared with LINKED nodes. Leave empty to disable.

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: SQLite database location. Default is recommended. Advanced users can use PostgreSQL: postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/bitbonsai

Config.Name: Allowed Origins
Config.Description: CORS allowed origins for API access. Use '*' for any origin (default), or comma-separated URLs for security (e.g., 'http://192.168.1.100:4210,http://nas.local:4210').

Config.Name: Media Paths
Config.Description: Comma-separated paths to scan for media files. Default scans both /media and /downloads. Add additional paths if mounting multiple libraries.

Config.Name: FFmpeg Binary
Config.Description: Custom FFmpeg binary path. Default 'ffmpeg' uses bundled version. Advanced users can specify custom builds (e.g., '/usr/local/bin/ffmpeg-custom').

Config.Name: Preview Screenshots
Config.Description: Enable automatic preview screenshot capture during encoding. Shows visual progress in web UI. Disable to save CPU/disk during encoding.

Config.Name: Extra Parameters
Config.Description: Additional Docker parameters. For NVIDIA GPU support, add: --runtime=nvidia --gpus all. For custom capabilities: --cap-add=SYS_ADMIN (required for NFS mounting).
